commands.review_summary:
The branch hardens trusted tool policy execution and before-tool-call diagnostics so unreadable or malformed policy registrations, decisions, params, and approvals are guarded and malformed cases block deterministically while valid undefined optional fields remain no-ops.

PR surface: Source +392, Tests +368. Total +760 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main directly reads trusted-policy registry, policy, and decision fields, while the PR adds synthetic contract cases that exercise those malformed paths. I did not execute tests because this review was required to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR is maintainer-labeled and deliberately changes compatibility-sensitive trusted-policy fail-closed runtime behavior.

target.title: fix(plugins): fail closed on trusted policy errors
target.body:
## Summary

- fails closed when trusted tool policy registration/list access, policy evaluation, decision fields, adjusted params, or approval payloads are unreadable/malformed
- routes before-tool-call diagnostics through the same guarded trusted-policy reader so unreadable policy ids cannot crash diagnostic/native policy state
- preserves valid no-op optional-field shapes like `{ allow: undefined }` and approval options set to `undefined`
- keeps the slice narrow to trusted policy execution/diagnostics plus contract coverage with synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures only

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 2 routed shards, 120 tests passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- touched-file scrub for the reported plugin/tool names, too-close synthetic suffixes, private paths, and private network literals: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean after the guarded diagnostic-reader follow-up
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: AWS Crabbox run `run_7024225182a8`, lease `cbx_01f6e94098e7`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`, exit 0 on head `eec15f25aa5`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: trusted tool policy failures no longer fail open or crash policy evaluation/diagnostic state when policy registries, registrations, decisions, params, approval payloads, or policy ids are malformed/unreadable; malformed concrete decisions block deterministically while valid optional `undefined` no-op fields remain compatible.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree rebased on current `origin/main`, plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate for the final squashed head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: squashed the PR back to one semantic commit, ran focused host-hooks contract and before-tool-call diagnostic tests, formatter check, diff check, scrub, autoreview until clean, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: contract coverage proves unreadable trusted policy lists/registrations/decisions/params/approval payloads and malformed concrete decisions return deterministic block results; agent diagnostics prove unreadable policy ids are reported through a fallback id instead of throwing; valid optional-field no-op decisions and approval payloads with undefined optional fields still pass through without denial.

Observed result after fix: a bad trusted-policy payload blocks only the unsafe tool call with a clear policy reason, and diagnostics can still describe the active policy set without crashing on unreadable policy metadata.

What was not tested: no live Telegram/UI round trip for this policy-only slice; runtime behavior is covered through plugin host-hook contract tests, before-tool-call diagnostic tests, autoreview, and the remote changed gate.
target.author: vincentkoc
